steps.title: Identify what makes judicial opinions strongest
steps.content: The passage's decisive clue is that “the strongest judicial opinions consider and rebut potential objections.” This defines Allen's criterion for evaluating judicial reasoning.

steps.title: Determine what discussing conflicting philosophers accomplishes
steps.content: Discussing philosophers with conflicting views improves arguments by requiring judges to address opposing perspectives. The other three contradict this logic or focus on unrelated goals. That matches choice B — B is correct.
